channel.title: +sAl on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +sAl on Vishvas's notes

item.title: boro gAro
item.description: gAro 30% of meghAlaya. In India, 90% are Christian (Presbyterians and Catholics), rest are hindu. koch and rAjbongshi Mostly hindu Assam and meghAlaya. The rAjbonshi became IA-ized. In the 16th century, the founder of the Koch dynasty, Bisu, adopted Hinduism, and called himself Biswa Singha. The 1871-1872 census of Assam shows Koch Rajbongshis to be the largest community in the state, coming at ~14%, Kacharis (not sure if this is only Bodo or it includes Dimasas, etc as well) at ~12%, Muslims were ~12%.
